afterDraft.title: Where to Park in Berlin near Alexanderplatz: Garages, P+R and Tourist Mistakes
imageAltTexts: A car beside an underground parking entrance near Alexanderplatz, with the Berlin TV Tower in the distance
The World Clock at Alexanderplatz, the meeting point visitors are usually trying to reach on time
A yellow U-Bahn train at Alexanderplatz station, a reminder that the car is not always the easiest central Berlin layer
A Berlin car-sharing vehicle parked on a city street, useful context for visitors who are comparing car, taxi and public transport choices
A mobility hub at Berlin Sudkreuz, showing how edge-of-centre transport choices can be easier than driving into the middle
afterDraft.coverAlt: A car beside an underground parking entrance near Alexanderplatz, with the Berlin TV Tower in the distance
